name: Abney, James Stewart
motto: "By Grannies," look at "Jim's" innocent face. It's beaming with Organic Pharmacognosy, and he hails from Toccopola. In his Junior year he branched off from the Lits onto Pharmacy, and gets two dips this year. "Jim" is quiet and reserved, but always has a smile. His mother used to tell him that he might be President, some day, and at last he has achieved his mother's ambition. "Ole Miss" will lose, this year, that excellent string of Abney boys, who have been here for years. We understand that "Jim" is the last of the brothers.

"Ole Miss" will lose, this year, that excellent string of Abney boys, who have been here for years. We understand that "Jim" is the last of the brothers.

name: Alderman, Manius Treadwell
motto: First in his class, first in the dining room, and first in the hearts of his fellow students. Greek was "Crip's" strong point, as long as Dr. "Decup" was here, because he was well versed in the campaigns around Holly Springs, in '65. Some day "Crip" will be a great planter, near Michigan City, where he hopes to make a success, raising boll weevils and boll worms. He is the only man in the class who expects to be a planter.
text: First in his class, first in the dining room, and first in the hearts of his fellow students. Greek was "Crip's" strong point, as long as Dr. "Decup" was here, because he was well versed in the campaigns around Holly Springs, in '65. Some day "Crip" will be a great planter, near Michigan City, where he hopes to make a success, raising boll weevils and boll worms. He is the only man in the class who expects to be a planter.

name: Alexander, Hugh Standifer
motto: A quiet, modest lad, peaceable and kindly disposed, with a heart filled with musical vibrations. "Buddie" has made himself very popular. Especially is this true with the occupants of Gordon Hall, where living will not be worth while next year without Hugh to render the music in the good old rag time way, while the rest are doing the Turkey Trot, the Bunny Hug, and the Grizzly Bear. There is no chance to get him back next year, for he says, "Shoot Man, I've got a girl."

There is no chance to get him back next year, for he says, "Shoot Man, I've got a girl."

name: Anderson, John Russell
motto: Webb School never sent a better student than John. He hails from the town made famous by John Allen's fish pond, and he has been telling fishy jokes ever since. Full of dry wit, rarely ever smiles at his jokes, and is happy as long as three "hots" come regular and a Blackstone is near. John is one of the most popular men in the class. If he is not in his room you will find him on the Tennis Court or in the Law Library. Webb upheld her reputation for scholarship when John arrived at "Ole Miss."

If he is not in his room you will find him on the Tennis Court or in the Law Library. Webb upheld her reputation for scholarship when John arrived at "Ole Miss."

name: Oliver, V. Austin
description: "Spout" is a graduate student you know that is in that dreadful land where travelers seldom return. Perhaps when he conquers this world, he will then enter the next for an L.L.D. degree. As a chemist, he rivals Sir Remus, and he has often made Ty Cobb ashamed of himself on the baseball field. Austin has become somewhat of a landmark around here and it will not look just right when he leaves.

As a chemist, he rivals Sir Remus, and he has often made Ty Cobb ashamed of himself on the baseball field. Austin has become somewhat of a landmark around here and it will not look just right when he leaves.

name: Quincy, Clarke Ayres
clubs: Secretary and Executive Committee Honor Council 1911-12
description: Quincy kills two birds at once this year—he gets a B.S. and B.E. at the same time. He intends to be an Engineer. We have been told that his first undertaking will be to dig a canal to communicate with the River Styx.
text: Quincy kills two birds at once this year—he gets a B.S. and B.E. at the same time. He intends to be an Engineer. We have been told that his first undertaking will be to dig a canal to communicate with the River Styx.

name: Laurie, Bailey
description: Miss Laurie was taught in her early youth that children should be seen and not heard, and she still adheres to that doctrine. However, this tendency to keep quiet does not indicate an inability to carry on an interesting conversation. Her stories are most remarkable. She possesses the ability to study and not rarely burns the midnight oil. She has made some very close friends, and there are none of the girls who do not love her.
text: Miss Laurie was taught in her early youth that children should be seen and not heard, and she still adheres to that doctrine. However, this tendency to keep quiet does not indicate an inability to carry on an interesting conversation. Her stories are most remarkable. She possesses the ability to study and not rarely burns the midnight oil. She has made some very close friends, and there are none of the girls who do not love her.

name: Julia, Baker
description: Miss Julia, the mischievous, the frolicsome, the fun-loving, is the girl who makes our work more pleasant and our lives brighter with her eternal puns, tricks and jokes. If she ever had a serious or melancholy thought no one ever knew it—to her friends and acquaintances, she is always bubbling over with fun. Despite all this, however, she finds time for study, and she stands very near the head of her class.
text: Miss Julia, the mischievous, the frolicsome, the fun-loving, is the girl who makes our work more pleasant and our lives brighter with her eternal puns, tricks and jokes. If she ever had a serious or melancholy thought no one ever knew it—to her friends and acquaintances, she is always bubbling over with fun. Despite all this, however, she finds time for study, and she stands very near the head of her class.

name: Bell, Ben Mosley
motto: "Consider the lilies of the field, they toil not, neither do they spin."
description: If Ben had his way about it he would, therefore, be a lily. Once upon a time Ben decided that a great Engineering future lay ahead of him. He deserted the Lits and joined the Surveyors, but the "inexorable logic" convinced him of his error and the last lap will be finished in the regular ranks. "She" lives at Coffeeville, and this is the hub of the Celestial Regions for Ben. He's a handsome young fellow and is well liked by his classmates.
text:
"Consider the lilies of the field, they toil not, neither do they spin."
If Ben had his way about it he would, therefore, be a lily. Once upon a time Ben decided that a great Engineering future lay ahead of him. He deserted the Lits and joined the Surveyors, but the "inexorable logic" convinced him of his error and the last lap will be finished in the regular ranks. "She" lives at Coffeeville, and this is the hub of the Celestial Regions for Ben. He's a handsome young fellow and is well liked by his classmates.

name: Bridges, Joseph G.
description: Professor Joseph G. Bridges is held in high esteem by his college chums, many of whom look up to him as a big brother and confidential adviser. When he speaks, it's on business. Like Joseph of old, he remains true to his brothers and is a thorough Christian man, with honest convictions. Perhaps a career as U. S. Commissioner of Education will be his if the Democrats are in control next fall. Anyhow, our prediction is that some day he will be heard from, and the noise he creates will be heard by the "Outer World," even though it originates at the far-distant Kossuth.

Anyhow, our prediction is that some day he will be heard from, and the noise he creates will be heard by the "Outer World," even though it originates at the far-distant Kossuth.

name: Bridgeforth, Allen
major: B.S., Sigma Alpha Epsilon, Sigma Kappa Beta, Taylor Medal in Zoology
description: Born on an old plantation, Allen came to "Ole Miss" from the Delta and joined the class of '12 in our Sophomore year. He is a brilliant student, a loyal friend and sincere in his convictions. On his dip will be tagged, "With distinction," and we rejoice that it is well earned. True to his early surroundings, he is daffy about Botany, and is writing his Thesis about the birds and trees, etc. He puts all his strength into an undertaking and the result is usually success.
text: Born on an old plantation, Allen came to "Ole Miss" from the Delta and joined the class of '12 in our Sophomore year. He is a brilliant student, a loyal friend and sincere in his convictions. On his dip will be tagged, "With distinction," and we rejoice that it is well earned. True to his early surroundings, he is daffy about Botany, and is writing his Thesis about the birds and trees, etc. He puts all his strength into an undertaking and the result is usually success.

name: Caldwell, Guy Alvin
major: B.S., Second Year Med., Columbia University
description: One down, one cigar; two down, two cigars; three down and a quarter. Vandy first, "Ole Miss" second, and Columbia third. Guy came to us when we were beginning the Sophomore year—and he began, too. The Scribs saw his worth and nabbed him, the Meds also nabbed, and then the Sigma Chis. Then came along Columbia needing a man to swell its eight thousand students, and they nabbed the very nabbable youth. We shall see something of Guy some day—if it is the dust from his heels.

Guy came to us when we were beginning the Sophomore year—and he began, too. The Scribs saw his worth and nabbed him, the Meds also nabbed, and then the Sigma Chis. Then came along Columbia needing a man to swell its eight thousand students, and they nabbed the very nabbable youth. We shall see something of Guy some day—if it is the dust from his heels.

name: Cameron, D. C.
description: This young gentleman came to us only a few weeks ago, and we think there must be hopes for him, for he left Millsaps in his fourth year and decided to make a stab for his diploma from the University. He was editor-in-chief of the Millsaps year book, and it is said that he is a good speaker, a good writer and a good student. He hails from the piney woods from which we have had many to hail within the past few years.
text: This young gentleman came to us only a few weeks ago, and we think there must be hopes for him, for he left Millsaps in his fourth year and decided to make a stab for his diploma from the University. He was editor-in-chief of the Millsaps year book, and it is said that he is a good speaker, a good writer and a good student. He hails from the piney woods from which we have had many to hail within the past few years.

name: Clark, Arthur Barnett
description: Arthur is true to his convictions, and is a friend of the kind which are much to be desired. He rooms with "Casey," and the heated arguments they have surely prove the truthfulness of this assertion. No one has anything against "Old Bonus," and, although he is familiarly termed "Bonehead" by his friends who know him best, he has a keen, active intellect and we predict that, ere long, he will be the leading attorney at the Ruleville bar, and Ruleville is strictly a prohibition town. Do not mistake our words.

Do not mistake our words.

name: Clifton, Sallie
description: Miss Sallie started out with the Lits, but entered the Law Class in her Junior year and devoted herself ardently to the intricacies of Blackstone. Remembering, however, that old friends are the best friends, she returned to the Academics and is now numbered in the class of 1912. Sallie tried I. I. & C. before coming here, and can tell wonderful tales of how they eat 'em alive over there. She possesses a strong personality, has a will of her own and is not easily led.

Remembering, however, that old friends are the best friends, she returned to the Academics and is now numbered in the class of 1912. Sallie tried I. I. & C. before coming here, and can tell wonderful tales of how they eat 'em alive over there. She possesses a strong personality, has a will of her own and is not easily led.

name: Cooper, Forest Graham
clubs: Secretary Reporter and President of Phi Sigma
description: "Coop" has left us very little space to say much about him. But we will add that he has been popular choice in his many honors and has faithfully performed each trust. If his present loyalty continues, in the future his Alma Mater will be benefitted by it. He expects—rather hopes—to instruct American youths next year.
text: "Coop" has left us very little space to say much about him. But we will add that he has been popular choice in his many honors and has faithfully performed each trust. If his present loyalty continues, in the future his Alma Mater will be benefitted by it. He expects—rather hopes—to instruct American youths next year.

name: Coxson, Claude F.
description: Irishly speaking, "Butch" is genuine, in looks, speech, and kinship with Mike Sennett. He revels in a good cigar and plenty of law books scattered around him. When "Butch" commands, the Freshies obey. Four years ago when this lad arrived he was somewhat different from what his picture now shows him to be. He has grown older, and then, other changes have been wrought. "Butch" is destined to a career of C. J. or J. P. some day, we don't know which. Or maybe he'll be the second St. Patrick to drive other snakes away.
text: Irishly speaking, "Butch" is genuine, in looks, speech, and kinship with Mike Sennett. He revels in a good cigar and plenty of law books scattered around him. He has grown older, and then, other changes have been wrought. "Butch" is destined to a career of C. J. or J. P. some day, we don't know which. Or maybe he'll be the second St. Patrick to drive other snakes away.

name: Flournoy, Richard W.
description: Dick believes that no College career is complete without a regular hunt each week for the feathered tribe—and he usually brings in the game. No student has any malice for Dick—all like him and when at last the intricacies of Blackstone have been learned from Uncle Tommy, a lawyer to the manor born, will leave "Ole Miss."
text: Dick believes that no College career is complete without a regular hunt each week for the feathered tribe—and he usually brings in the game. No student has any malice for Dick—all like him and when at last the intricacies of Blackstone have been learned from Uncle Tommy, a lawyer to the manor born, will leave "Ole Miss."

name: Fuller, Will Lindsay
description: "Judge Beckmet" sampled L. I. I. and Jefferson Military Academy before coming to "Ole Miss" four years ago, and incidentally got a good preparation thrown in. His main studies there were playing checkers and now he holds the University Championship. Intelligent, versatile, an admirer of beauty and art, with a kind and amiable manner, never losing his temper, he will leave a clean record behind him, and may expect a brilliant prospect ahead. Will teach the kids a few years before making his legal reputation.
text: "Judge Beckmet" sampled L. I. I. and Jefferson Military Academy before coming to "Ole Miss" four years ago, and incidentally got a good preparation thrown in. His main studies there were playing checkers and now he holds the University Championship. Intelligent, versatile, an admirer of beauty and art, with a kind and amiable manner, never losing his temper, he will leave a clean record behind him, and may expect a brilliant prospect ahead. Will teach the kids a few years before making his legal reputation.

name: Hardy, John Allison
description: This lover came from somewhere down in the bottoms of the great Tombigbee, where the catamounts howl, and would doubtless have made a good student had Dan Cupid not wounded him so severely in the beginning of his College career—he may recover, but we have our doubts. His grades are creditable, even though, to be truthful, we must admit that he does not sit night after night and bear his eyes with books. Few know him, but those who are fortunate enough to enjoy an intimate acquaintance, prize his friendship highly.

His grades are creditable, even though, to be truthful, we must admit that he does not sit night after night and bear his eyes with books. Few know him, but those who are fortunate enough to enjoy an intimate acquaintance, prize his friendship highly.

name: Harper, Robert Blackburn
description: "Black" was here as a Sophomore when we were collegiate babies, but he played truant one year and had to fall back to the '12 class. He is quiet, unassuming, studies hard, and is unfailing as a friend. Harper will secure his certificate in Medicine this term and hopes to become a full-fledged Medical Doctor in two more years—then he will start his graveyard unless, having been a pharmacist for some time, he has already started his and will continue to use the same one.
text: "Black" was here as a Sophomore when we were collegiate babies, but he played truant one year and had to fall back to the '12 class. He is quiet, unassuming, studies hard, and is unfailing as a friend. Harper will secure his certificate in Medicine this term and hopes to become a full-fledged Medical Doctor in two more years—then he will start his graveyard unless, having been a pharmacist for some time, he has already started his and will continue to use the same one.

name: Holliman, Thomas Hunkle
description: Hink leads them all on the light fantastic toe, not the waltz, but the clog variety, and there are fifty-seven of them. He says that I. O. B. H. stands for I ought to be hung, but we don't agree with him, for a great future is ahead of him on the stage, at least so long as scenery is not moved by machinery altogether. He precipitated into society last February for the first time when the Jones County Club entertained itself, and it proved the last experiment with precipitating for Hink.

H. stands for I ought to be hung, but we don't agree with him, for a great future is ahead of him on the stage, at least so long as scenery is not moved by machinery altogether. He precipitated into society last February for the first time when the Jones County Club entertained itself, and it proved the last experiment with precipitating for Hink.

name: Jones, J. Ivy
description: Perhaps holds one record which will stand for many years—the Senior who has spoken the fewest words in the four years of his college life. Chemistry is his long suit, and his hobby is to wear good clothes. He has the strange honor of being the only Jones in the class—an unusual thing, this being one of the seven wonders of the world.
text: Perhaps holds one record which will stand for many years—the Senior who has spoken the fewest words in the four years of his college life. Chemistry is his long suit, and his hobby is to wear good clothes. He has the strange honor of being the only Jones in the class—an unusual thing, this being one of the seven wonders of the world.

name: Kyle, John W.
description: Known to a large majority of the boys as the best student in school, John carries his well-won dignity like a man. It will probably be long in the future before Ole Miss will have another John Kyle. Not a seeker after honors by any means, John has bagged several in his four years here. He expects to be of service to the criminals around Batesville, but he is just itching to tackle Uncle Tommy's law before doing gymnastic stunts before Panola County juries.
text: Known to a large majority of the boys as the best student in school, John carries his well-won dignity like a man. It will probably be long in the future before Ole Miss will have another John Kyle. Not a seeker after honors by any means, John has bagged several in his four years here. He expects to be of service to the criminals around Batesville, but he is just itching to tackle Uncle Tommy's law before doing gymnastic stunts before Panola County juries.

name: Leavell, Clarence Stanley
description: "Jug" is the "out-hustliest hustler" at the University, and he has in him the stuff which spells success. Many might profit by the example if they would consider the pluck and vim with which this Oxford lad attacks a proposition, and withal, he combines an ability to give sober consideration to every undertaking. He has upheld the Leavell name and the burden has certainly been great, for that family has had a representative here, sometimes three or four, since the early ages.

He has upheld the Leavell name and the burden has certainly been great, for that family has had a representative here, sometimes three or four, since the early ages.

name: Lundie, Annie Belle
description: Being one of the two co-eds from Oxford to be dipped this year, Miss Annie Belle seems to like the honor well. She has, by her independence, pleasing smile and well-balanced dignity, won the admiration of all who know her. She may teach next year if Master Cupid can be persuaded to leave Oxford.
text: Being one of the two co-eds from Oxford to be dipped this year, Miss Annie Belle seems to like the honor well. She has, by her independence, pleasing smile and well-balanced dignity, won the admiration of all who know her. She may teach next year if Master Cupid can be persuaded to leave Oxford.

name: McLean, John Hawkins
description: John is a sober-minded, serious sort of a fellow, but he likes a joke, and, although we believe that he will make a shining light in the legal world, yet we feel that when he decided to take up the study of law the American stage lost an excellent black-face comedian. Our only hope is that he may be as successful in all his undertakings in the future as he has been in the past in the playing of certain jokes, to which many a wayfarer may testify.
text: John is a sober-minded, serious sort of a fellow, but he likes a joke, and, although we believe that he will make a shining light in the legal world, yet we feel that when he decided to take up the study of law the American stage lost an excellent black-face comedian. Our only hope is that he may be as successful in all his undertakings in the future as he has been in the past in the playing of certain jokes, to which many a wayfarer may testify.

name: Mitchell, Steve Frank
description: Captain Mitchell, aside from being an athlete of the first rank, is a student, and is almost universally liked. He is kind-hearted, conscientious and true, and it is with much regret on the part of the student body that Steve graduates this year. His mature judgment was shown in early youth, when he refused to be sent to the Agricultural College with his brothers, but came to us. Mitchell is studying law now, and says that he expects to amount to much.
text: Captain Mitchell, aside from being an athlete of the first rank, is a student, and is almost universally liked. He is kind-hearted, conscientious and true, and it is with much regret on the part of the student body that Steve graduates this year. His mature judgment was shown in early youth, when he refused to be sent to the Agricultural College with his brothers, but came to us. Mitchell is studying law now, and says that he expects to amount to much.

name: Pool, Winston Cam
description: This lad comes from Leakesville wherever that is but don't blame him. Perhaps that is why he is one of the "Unlucky Trio." That village will be famous some day as the "Village in which Carl Pool lived." He is never without his "Star navy" and "Prince Albert." If a sweet, young lady should smile at Carl you might think he had delirium tremens. A good, easy, genial fellow, who does not mix. C. H. A. sent him here four years ago with a wonderful reputation. He has sustained both the wonder and the reputation.

He has sustained both the wonder and the reputation.

name: Reedy, Annie
description: She is one of the most conscientious girls that ever came to Ole Miss, sometimes even to a fault. There is nothing that she will not do for her friends. She is so obliging that her friends work her—but if she suspects it the game is up. She has a quick temper, and sometimes pouts about a trifle. Chief of Second Floor Detective Agency, she is a renowned sleuth, working out the most baffling cases with an ease and ability that would have made Sherlock Holmes green with envy.
text: She is one of the most conscientious girls that ever came to Ole Miss, sometimes even to a fault. There is nothing that she will not do for her friends. She is so obliging that her friends work her—but if she suspects it the game is up. She has a quick temper, and sometimes pouts about a trifle. Chief of Second Floor Detective Agency, she is a renowned sleuth, working out the most baffling cases with an ease and ability that would have made Sherlock Holmes green with envy.

name: Rhodes, Marguerite B.
description: Miss Marguerite is certainly the most popular girl in school—with both the boys and the girls—for no one who knows her can help loving her, and others admire her from the distance. Possessing sufficient propriety, charming manners and friendly smiles for everyone, she will easily become a leader in the social world. She doesn't study hard, for she knows how to "Bug" the Profs., and so gets through creditably.
text: Miss Marguerite is certainly the most popular girl in school—with both the boys and the girls—for no one who knows her can help loving her, and others admire her from the distance. Possessing sufficient propriety, charming manners and friendly smiles for everyone, she will easily become a leader in the social world. She doesn't study hard, for she knows how to "Bug" the Profs., and so gets through creditably.

name: Rowland, Peter Whitman Jr.
description: Some have said that "Whit" is proud that he lives in Oxford, but we don't make any such charge. He has been a college "Rah Rah" from his earliest babyhood, and is on to the tricks thereof. He is sometimes called "Genius" the why for which we cannot explain. His favorite pastime is swapping yarns and imitating his friends and pal, "All right for that one." "Whit's" ability is unquestionable, but we do sometimes doubt his energy.

He is sometimes called "Genius" the why for which we cannot explain. His favorite pastime is swapping yarns and imitating his friends and pal, "All right for that one." "Whit's" ability is unquestionable, but we do sometimes doubt his energy.

name: Rucker, Robert Bedford
description: R. B. is a good student and his ability is well seen from the fact that he captures two dips this year, although he has been here only four years. He will probably go into partnership with the undertaker when he finishes two more years in Med.
text: R. B. is a good student and his ability is well seen from the fact that he captures two dips this year, although he has been here only four years. He will probably go into partnership with the undertaker when he finishes two more years in Med.

name: Slay, Ronald J.
description: Immediately upon his election as presiding officer of our class in its Freshman year he was dubbed "President," and he has retained that proud cognomen ever since—yea, from early youth. He hails from down where they are sure enough bad, but somehow he is a peaceable sort of a fellow, and being very much in love, he has little time for anything else. We feel safe in saying that Lamar County is well represented in the Halls of the University.
text: Immediately upon his election as presiding officer of our class in its Freshman year he was dubbed "President," and he has retained that proud cognomen ever since—yea, from early youth. He hails from down where they are sure enough bad, but somehow he is a peaceable sort of a fellow, and being very much in love, he has little time for anything else. We feel safe in saying that Lamar County is well represented in the Halls of the University.

name: Sims, Claudia Lee
description: MISS Claudia is very pleasant. She has a well-balanced temper and a most peculiar taste for Math., as the Engineering Quartette will testify. Surely the Mathematics room will be lonesome for many years after her departure, for seldom do our co-eds take more than the required five hours in this line. Miss Sims is a good student, will graduate near the head of her class, and she is one of the three girls who have won Taylor Medals.

Miss Sims is a good student, will graduate near the head of her class, and she is one of the three girls who have won Taylor Medals.

name: Stephenson, Daniel Duff, Jr.
description: Yes, that's him. Look at his picture. He's our President. Well, we are proud of him, even if he is from Columbus. Webb turned loose three years ago, and seeing nothing else to join, he joined our class and he has been joining everything since. He is a Latin and Greek shark and once upon a time he told a new joke and got up for breakfast on time. In the "Ole Miss" contest for the best joke, "Duff" was very prominently mentioned. Few in the "Dozen" class have a better chance for success than "Duff." He expects to have a corner on cotton some day.
text: Yes, that's him. Look at his picture. He's our President. Well, we are proud of him, even if he is from Columbus. Webb turned loose three years ago, and seeing nothing else to join, he joined our class and he has been joining everything since. He is a Latin and Greek shark and once upon a time he told a new joke and got up for breakfast on time. In the "Ole Miss" contest for the best joke, "Duff" was very prominently mentioned. Few in the "Dozen" class have a better chance for success than "Duff." He expects to have a corner on cotton some day.

name: Trotter, William Chamberlain
description: Between Freshman Math. and under Uncle Tommy, Chuck is in a worse predicament than he was when he found that A. & M. line long years ago. However, he is a hustler now, as he was then.
text: Between Freshman Math. and under Uncle Tommy, Chuck is in a worse predicament than he was when he found that A. & M. line long years ago. However, he is a hustler now, as he was then.

name: Watkins, Ruth
description: Miss Ruth is the M.A. in the Hall and consequently the information bureau for all the girls. She is passionately fond of "On Mobile Bay," although she spends most of her time singing, "I'm Papa's Sweetheart," much to the amusement of her friends. She is a most appreciative listener and never fails to laugh when it is time, consequently she is very popular. This will most likely be her last year with us.
text: Miss Ruth is the M.A. in the Hall and consequently the information bureau for all the girls. She is passionately fond of "On Mobile Bay," although she spends most of her time singing, "I'm Papa's Sweetheart," much to the amusement of her friends. She is a most appreciative listener and never fails to laugh when it is time, consequently she is very popular. This will most likely be her last year with us.

name: White, Joe Paul
clubs: Chaplain and President of Phi Sigma
description: "Red" is perhaps the champion worker in his class. Two years ago Junior Law and about thirty-five hours Lit were his. Last year about ninety hours of Lit fell before the mighty hunter. This year "Uncle Tommy's" law has been keeping him quiet. This lad from Leake—this Bugger of Profs.—takes, seizes and appropriates to his own use one white parchment, tagged B.S. and marked "Exhibit A" in his record, and also another aforesaid diploma marked, LL.B., which said parchment appears of record in "Book BB" in the Chancellor's office. He is now a candidate for an LL.D.
text: "Red" is perhaps the champion worker in his class. Two years ago Junior Law and about thirty-five hours Lit were his. Last year about ninety hours of Lit fell before the mighty hunter. This year "Uncle Tommy's" law has been keeping him quiet. This lad from Leake—this Bugger of Profs.—takes, seizes and appropriates to his own use one white parchment, tagged B.S. and marked "Exhibit A" in his record, and also another aforesaid diploma marked, LL.B., which said parchment appears of record in "Book BB" in the Chancellor's office. He is now a candidate for an LL.D.

name: Winter, Jeva
description: A quiet, congenial lad, true to his friends, noted for his independence. His chief delight is observing how many degrees centigrade a piece of ice cast into a cup of boiling water will cause the temperature to change.
text: A quiet, congenial lad, true to his friends, noted for his independence. His chief delight is observing how many degrees centigrade a piece of ice cast into a cup of boiling water will cause the temperature to change.

name: Ayres, Sam Nathan Jr.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Junior Law Class '11
Secretary and Treasurer Ole Miss Board of Directors '12
description: Small in size, but larger in the estimation of his fellow students—a hard fighter to carry his points, but a cheerful loser if such falls to his share. He has a pun to suit any occasion and attempts to "put one over" on the "Senior Lawyer's Dread" at times. He has made good in his two years' stay at the University.
text: Small in size, but larger in the estimation of his fellow students—a hard fighter to carry his points, but a cheerful loser if such falls to his share. He has a pun to suit any occasion and attempts to "put one over" on the "Senior Lawyer's Dread" at times. He has made good in his two years' stay at the University.

name: Blackwell, Morris Glenn
clubs: Board of Directors of Ole Miss
description: Blackwell used to be a lover of light literature, but about the time of the second term exams of his Senior year he came to the conclusion that such "literature was not legal," so he transferred his affections to such solid substance as "Pomeroy's Equity" and "Conflict of Laws." He likewise is a strong Blackstone Clubber and his speeches in that august body will be ringing in echo from Lamar Hall in years to come. When he "dips" his right to fame at the close of this year he will follow Grady's injunction and "Go South to win fortune."
text: Blackwell used to be a lover of light literature, but about the time of the second term exams of his Senior year he came to the conclusion that such "literature was not legal," so he transferred his affections to such solid substance as "Pomeroy's Equity" and "Conflict of Laws." He likewise is a strong Blackstone Clubber and his speeches in that august body will be ringing in echo from Lamar Hall in years to come. When he "dips" his right to fame at the close of this year he will follow Grady's injunction and "Go South to win fortune."

name: Boggan, T. K.
description: A man well read and well versed in the affairs of this world is our friend. After graduating in the literary department a few years ago, Boggan taught school at Collins and Biloxi. He is one of the "happy married quartette" whose names appear on our class rolls. He is a good thinker and has the power of clear expression, both of which attributes are very much to be desired by a lawyer. His "starving period" as a young lawyer should not last very long.
text: A man well read and well versed in the affairs of this world is our friend. After graduating in the literary department a few years ago, Boggan taught school at Collins and Biloxi. He is one of the "happy married quartette" whose names appear on our class rolls. He is a good thinker and has the power of clear expression, both of which attributes are very much to be desired by a lawyer. His "starving period" as a young lawyer should not last very long.

name: Brown, James Thomas
description: "Casey" blew in from the Old Dominion State, and believing that Mississippi was a good place to camp, "that is to say," live, he settled down to legal toils. If he survives the year's grind he will make his mark at the bar, for he has a clear perception and that blustering emphativeness of his is sure to take in an unsuspecting juror.
text: "Casey" blew in from the Old Dominion State, and believing that Mississippi was a good place to camp, "that is to say," live, he settled down to legal toils. If he survives the year's grind he will make his mark at the bar, for he has a clear perception and that blustering emphativeness of his is sure to take in an unsuspecting juror.

name: Buckley, Jack Emmett
description: This "Enterprising" young fellow had an idea that he was fond of the study of law until Equity Jurisprudence came into his course, then all earlier ambitions vanished and he no longer aspires to be a lawyer, but a barrister. Buck is a good student, and this, with his practical ideas, speaks well for his future. An intimate association is necessary for a full appreciation of his jovial disposition.
text: This "Enterprising" young fellow had an idea that he was fond of the study of law until Equity Jurisprudence came into his course, then all earlier ambitions vanished and he no longer aspires to be a lawyer, but a barrister. Buck is a good student, and this, with his practical ideas, speaks well for his future. An intimate association is necessary for a full appreciation of his jovial disposition.

name: Carter, Fred Spengler
description: This big fellow hails from the "icy north," and he tells some very marvelous stories about what happened down on the farm and about how he operated the Acme Automatic. "Freddie" is a good athlete and can study when he feels so inclined. He was the man who came within three yards of tying up the Thanksgiving game and then spent a month in bed. He is a hero, therefore. He is well known and well liked, from the Chancellor's office to the Hospital.
text: This big fellow hails from the "icy north," and he tells some very marvelous stories about what happened down on the farm and about how he operated the Acme Automatic. "Freddie" is a good athlete and can study when he feels so inclined. He was the man who came within three yards of tying up the Thanksgiving game and then spent a month in bed. He is a hero, therefore. He is well known and well liked, from the Chancellor's office to the Hospital.

name: Cleveland, Arthur T.
description: The "Judge" hails from the county without a railroad, but then railroads don't make good lawyers, especially the kind that we are sure "Grover" Cleveland will make. He is death on technical points and often times quotes the very words of the author, yea, quotes them even to the satisfaction of our "dear Uncle T." This fellow is a hard worker and things once understood by him are never forgotten.
text: The "Judge" hails from the county without a railroad, but then railroads don't make good lawyers, especially the kind that we are sure "Grover" Cleveland will make. He is death on technical points and often times quotes the very words of the author, yea, quotes them even to the satisfaction of our "dear Uncle T." This fellow is a hard worker and things once understood by him are never forgotten.

name: Conner, Martin Sennett
clubs: Vice-President and Critic Hermaean Y. M. C. A. Cabinet '08-'11
Toastmaster Y. M. C. A. and Hermaean Banquets
President F. S. O. S. P.
description: Proud of his Irish ancestry, "Mike" is the most appropriate name, and he lives it out every inch. Born to lead, he has not only done so, but has been twice voted "Man most likely to succeed." "Mike" has bagged perhaps more honors than any other man, and stands one of the most popular among us. Kind, loyal, true, hospitable, energetic, ambitious, broad-minded, and a Christian gentleman, he leaves Ole Miss with the best wishes of both students and faculty.
text: Proud of his Irish ancestry, "Mike" is the most appropriate name, and he lives it out every inch. Born to lead, he has not only done so, but has been twice voted "Man most likely to succeed." "Mike" has bagged perhaps more honors than any other man, and stands one of the most popular among us. Kind, loyal, true, hospitable, energetic, ambitious, broad-minded, and a Christian gentleman, he leaves Ole Miss with the best wishes of both students and faculty.

name: Dean, Silas L.
description: Though calmly unconscious of it all, this important "limb" of the postal service is the one who pigeon-holes "the hopes, the fears, the joys and tears" of each member of our college community. With the ladies, Si's charms are irresistible, and among the boys his friends consist of all those who know him. Whether it's law or insurance, ten years hence, Dearic can lend us all funds.
text: Though calmly unconscious of it all, this important "limb" of the postal service is the one who pigeon-holes "the hopes, the fears, the joys and tears" of each member of our college community. With the ladies, Si's charms are irresistible, and among the boys his friends consist of all those who know him. Whether it's law or insurance, ten years hence, Dearic can lend us all funds.

name: Hosey, George William
description: Billy is a lawyer, but we are inclined to think that this profession is a second choice with him. He went to A. & M. first and came away with the "Corn." Farming was too prosaic and plodding for such an ambitious and gifted son of the soil, so the wayfarer hitched himself on the "Soft, Soft Medical" wagon which we feel sure will some day land him in Glory Land.
text: Billy is a lawyer, but we are inclined to think that this profession is a second choice with him. He went to A. & M. first and came away with the "Corn." Farming was too prosaic and plodding for such an ambitious and gifted son of the soil, so the wayfarer hitched himself on the "Soft, Soft Medical" wagon which we feel sure will some day land him in Glory Land.

name: Hoskins, John Stone
description: "Johnnie's" opinion often differs from that of the author, but he fearlessly defends his views. He has ideas of his own and the verbosity to establish them in the minds of the unsuspecting and the unsophisticated. His prospects for success are so very apparent that he even sees them himself, and will not hesitate to tell you how rosy the future will be. He has one hundred and thirty pounds of energy and his capacity for work has no limit.

He has one hundred and thirty pounds of energy and his capacity for work has no limit.

name: Jomys, Robert Anrurn
description: "Rags" is a good scout, and everybody likes him. He is "some class" as an athlete, and business manager. "Ole Miss" indicates what his associates think of his business ability. The word "lady" occurs frequently in his working vocabulary, and although law heads the list, we know it is only a stepping-stone to greatness.
text: "Rags" is a good scout, and everybody likes him. He is "some class" as an athlete, and business manager. "Ole Miss" indicates what his associates think of his business ability. The word "lady" occurs frequently in his working vocabulary, and although law heads the list, we know it is only a stepping-stone to greatness.

name: Wade, James Patrick
description: LeRoy's renown as a forensic orator is very great. Often times we have collapsed before his vociferous criticisms which he dispensed in Blackstone Club meetings without fear or favor. It is not recorded that "Pat's" two years' course here will result in, either to him or to the University. He has the true Irish wit and often times has entertained the Seniors in class assembled with one of his dialogues with our presiding officer.
text: LeRoy's renown as a forensic orator is very great. Often times we have collapsed before his vociferous criticisms which he dispensed in Blackstone Club meetings without fear or favor. It is not recorded that "Pat's" two years' course here will result in, either to him or to the University. He has the true Irish wit and often times has entertained the Seniors in class assembled with one of his dialogues with our presiding officer.

name: Pierce, Millard F.
description: Though claiming no close relationship to Venus, "Monk" will doubtless make a staunch citizen of Newton. His time has been well spent in the University, and he has exhibited versatile ability; standing well in his classes and also assisting in bringing to his Alma Mater athletic laurels, having served her efficiently as basketball manager.
text: Though claiming no close relationship to Venus, "Monk" will doubtless make a staunch citizen of Newton. His time has been well spent in the University, and he has exhibited versatile ability; standing well in his classes and also assisting in bringing to his Alma Mater athletic laurels, having served her efficiently as basketball manager.

name: Rayburn, Stevens Banks
description: Rayburn enjoys the distinction of being the smallest known organism in existence with a University degree. But despite this fact, the noise that he makes in debate deafens those near and fills those afar with a fear of his wrath. His ambition soars even to the administration of justice in a "J. P." court, and his grades indicate a realization of his dreams.
text: Rayburn enjoys the distinction of being the smallest known organism in existence with a University degree. But despite this fact, the noise that he makes in debate deafens those near and fills those afar with a fear of his wrath. His ambition soars even to the administration of justice in a "J. P." court, and his grades indicate a realization of his dreams.

name: Schalber, Ambrose Barney
description: Evidently when "Dick" entered the University he came with a determination to take all that she could confer upon an energetic son. Since that time he has taken an active part in the life of his Alma Mater, taking degree after degree until there is no other to seek. His preparation is full and his foundation good, and there seems to be nothing to bar his future success.
text: Evidently when "Dick" entered the University he came with a determination to take all that she could confer upon an energetic son. Since that time he has taken an active part in the life of his Alma Mater, taking degree after degree until there is no other to seek. His preparation is full and his foundation good, and there seems to be nothing to bar his future success.

name: Smith, Thomas T.
description: Upon his entrance four years ago Tom applied for a degree in Literature and football, but the loafing spirit struck him and for the past two years he has been on the legal "gravy train." He is often present with the merry midnight crew, but when the fun is over. A very advanced round in the ladder of success awaits this son of Lincoln.
text:
"the Grind" is his motto.
Upon his entrance four years ago Tom applied for a degree in Literature and football, but the loafing spirit struck him and for the past two years he has been on the legal "gravy train." He is often present with the merry midnight crew, but when the fun is over. A very advanced round in the ladder of success awaits this son of Lincoln.

name: Vardaman, James Money
description: The Lord didn't do much for him in the way of beauty, but he made up for it in qualities far more valuable. He is a cheer leader of great worth, a good student, and in all very popular among those who know him. He has been with us a long time, and we will all miss him next year. May his future be as great as his course here has been creditable.
text: The Lord didn't do much for him in the way of beauty, but he made up for it in qualities far more valuable. He is a cheer leader of great worth, a good student, and in all very popular among those who know him. He has been with us a long time, and we will all miss him next year. May his future be as great as his course here has been creditable.

name: White, J. Paul
description: Red, White, and sometimes Blue, when he doesn't relate exactly the right kind of legal doctrines, is a patriotic student of the law and this of itself means that he will take high rank in his chosen profession. True, it is that he has a threatening manner, but we speak advisedly, he is as harmless as distant thunder on a summer's night. We refer you to other pages for further personal advertising.
text: Red, White, and sometimes Blue, when he doesn't relate exactly the right kind of legal doctrines, is a patriotic student of the law and this of itself means that he will take high rank in his chosen profession. True, it is that he has a threatening manner, but we speak advisedly, he is as harmless as distant thunder on a summer's night. We refer you to other pages for further personal advertising.

name: Ayers, Quincy Cluver
description: When the Honorable Court of St. James was organized, an electrical engineer was needed to do the electrocuting, and Quincy got the job and made his first money. President Quincy of the Engineering Association is the best president they have ever had. A fine boy, popular and true to his friends. He has an evenly balanced temper, a kind heart, a good mind, and we predict that if he will apply himself diligently to the tasks before him and will play the part of a politician, he will have some chance of being elected county surveyor of Lowndes in the days to come.
text: When the Honorable Court of St. James was organized, an electrical engineer was needed to do the electrocuting, and Quincy got the job and made his first money. President Quincy of the Engineering Association is the best president they have ever had. A fine boy, popular and true to his friends. He has an evenly balanced temper, a kind heart, a good mind, and we predict that if he will apply himself diligently to the tasks before him and will play the part of a politician, he will have some chance of being elected county surveyor of Lowndes in the days to come.

name: Dunbar, Gussie A.
motto: "By guns, I'll do it," says he.
description: Gussie is the most handsome one of the famous surveying quartette getting diplomas this year. Look at his face. It is the very picture of independence. He especially likes Soph Math., which is very easy to him—the great mathematical mind that he has. Confident, self-possessed, with some engineering experience, he has an excellent opportunity of making good. His first work will be to dig a tunnel through the earth to China.
text:
"By guns, I'll do it," says he.
Gussie is the most handsome one of the famous surveying quartette getting diplomas this year. Look at his face. It is the very picture of independence. He especially likes Soph Math., which is very easy to him—the great mathematical mind that he has. Confident, self-possessed, with some engineering experience, he has an excellent opportunity of making good. His first work will be to dig a tunnel through the earth to China.

name: Eades, William Richards
description: The reason that Eades has red hair and is a good student is that he was here when the memory of man runneth not to the contrary. Hopes some day to build an electric line from Oxford to Mars and he usually succeeds in everything he tries. Several years ago the engineering profession needed "Red" and he unselfishly heeded the call, left Ole Miss and made his reputation. This year he came back to get the finishing touches.
text: The reason that Eades has red hair and is a good student is that he was here when the memory of man runneth not to the contrary. Hopes some day to build an electric line from Oxford to Mars and he usually succeeds in everything he tries. Several years ago the engineering profession needed "Red" and he unselfishly heeded the call, left Ole Miss and made his reputation. This year he came back to get the finishing touches.

name: Landsey, John Jr.
description: From Jones County and still a right respectable boy. He hopes to be an engineer some day, and we guess he will follow in the footsteps of his father and invent another eight-wheel wagon to haul his diploma away in. Since his club elected him treasurer, we admit that he is honest. Being the president of his class, "the wonder was and still the wonder grew, that one small head could carry all he knew."
text: From Jones County and still a right respectable boy. He hopes to be an engineer some day, and we guess he will follow in the footsteps of his father and invent another eight-wheel wagon to haul his diploma away in. Since his club elected him treasurer, we admit that he is honest. Being the president of his class, "the wonder was and still the wonder grew, that one small head could carry all he knew."

name: Jones, Cunningham Adams.
description: Just turn to the Athletic department and read the wonderful account of "Red," then go to Dr. Nicely and ask about his great medical ability; next go to Rube Barker, who will tell you what a good roommate he is. There, you have a good picture of this big boy, big in body, heart and soul. Captain "Red" received his preparatory training at Millsaps and then came to the larger fields near Oxford, where he has developed into somewhat of a society youth during his old days. He will be back to soak the farmers next year.
text: Just turn to the Athletic department and read the wonderful account of "Red," then go to Dr. Nicely and ask about his great medical ability; next go to Rube Barker, who will tell you what a good roommate he is. There, you have a good picture of this big boy, big in body, heart and soul. Captain "Red" received his preparatory training at Millsaps and then came to the larger fields near Oxford, where he has developed into somewhat of a society youth during his old days. He will be back to soak the farmers next year.

name: Barker, Rube.
clubs: "Ole Miss" Representative in Madison Square Garden Track Meet 1912
description: She, who wins his heart, has much to be proud of. A perfect athlete, blessed with a wonderful physique, "Dr. Rube," appropriately named gentlemen, friend, companion, is one of the most popular men in the University. Over forty medals have been awarded to "Rube" for track records and he holds a few Southern ones, by the way, and was almost unanimously chosen All-Southern Football Lineman. But still he's just plain old "Rube," not conciliated one bit.
text: She, who wins his heart, has much to be proud of. A perfect athlete, blessed with a wonderful physique, "Dr. Rube," appropriately named gentlemen, friend, companion, is one of the most popular men in the University. Over forty medals have been awarded to "Rube" for track records and he holds a few Southern ones, by the way, and was almost unanimously chosen All-Southern Football Lineman. But still he's just plain old "Rube," not conciliated one bit.

name: Brown, George Andrew.
description: George intends to be an M.D. some day, and, doubtless he will be in a position to render valuable services to the undertaker. However, by his quiet, friendly manner he has shown no signs of enmity to his fellow man yet. He is a warm friend to those who know him.
text: George intends to be an M.D. some day, and, doubtless he will be in a position to render valuable services to the undertaker. However, by his quiet, friendly manner he has shown no signs of enmity to his fellow man yet. He is a warm friend to those who know him.

name: Burckhardt, Fann.
description: The wonder was, and still the wonder grew, How one small arm could carry all the books it do. He says that the way that he has learned so much is by absorbing from the library under his arm. Our Senior Classes seem to have robbed Attala of the cream of her manly products, and here's one of the richest of them all. Birchy is dangerously associated with love, he rooms with love, walks with love, and studies with love. He is never happier than when telling of his sojourn in Mexico, which, as we are informed, was painfully brief and hurriedly ended.
text: The wonder was, and still the wonder grew, How one small arm could carry all the books it do. He says that the way that he has learned so much is by absorbing from the library under his arm. Our Senior Classes seem to have robbed Attala of the cream of her manly products, and here's one of the richest of them all. Birchy is dangerously associated with love, he rooms with love, walks with love, and studies with love. He is never happier than when telling of his sojourn in Mexico, which, as we are informed, was painfully brief and hurriedly ended.

name: Bell, James Canothers
description: Jim is the only one of the "West Pointers" who has decided to study Medicine, but then Jim will fix enough for the whole bunch. You would never guess from his military tread that he is a graduate of West Point (High), but so he is. He is a good student, but finds time to spend with his friends, who all are free, though Jim's future is a little hazy now that the sunshine will drive away the clouds and success will be his.
text: Jim is the only one of the "West Pointers" who has decided to study Medicine, but then Jim will fix enough for the whole bunch. You would never guess from his military tread that he is a graduate of West Point (High), but so he is. He is a good student, but finds time to spend with his friends, who all are free, though Jim's future is a little hazy now that the sunshine will drive away the clouds and success will be his.

name: Farley, David Laborve
description: Our mind is replete with good things when we think of "Booze." He has been here five years already and may continue his sojourn for some time yet. His one regret is that Junior Law does not count toward a medical degree. Farley is a good student, a good writer, and an all-around good fellow. We expect him to prove to be a great scientist and to discover some new microbe to threaten our health and prosperity.
text: Our mind is replete with good things when we think of "Booze." He has been here five years already and may continue his sojourn for some time yet. His one regret is that Junior Law does not count toward a medical degree. Farley is a good student, a good writer, and an all-around good fellow. We expect him to prove to be a great scientist and to discover some new microbe to threaten our health and prosperity.

name: Garloway, James H.
description: After making hay while the sun was shining down at Millsaps College, and banking up all of his knowledge, Jim struck a trot for "Ole Miss," where he decided to be a physician like his dad. Jim has girls all of the way from Mississippi City to Oxford, and every one of them thinks that he is in love with her. He especially likes medical jurisprudence, is very fond of Anatomy, glories when the Chemistry Class comes, and shouts when exams are near. In fact, with a little effort, Jim will make a good physician.

Jim has girls all of the way from Mississippi City to Oxford, and every one of them thinks that he is in love with her. He especially likes medical jurisprudence, is very fond of Anatomy, glories when the Chemistry Class comes, and shouts when exams are near. In fact, with a little effort, Jim will make a good physician.

name: Hammond, H. G.
description: A good, solid fellow is our friend, Hammond. He is very charitable and will give his life to the service of his fellow man and incidentally to make a little coin along with the charitable services. May he have abundant service.
text: A good, solid fellow is our friend, Hammond. He is very charitable and will give his life to the service of his fellow man and incidentally to make a little coin along with the charitable services. May he have abundant service.

name: Harper, Robert Blackwell
description: "Black" is "one of them things" in the art of Jim Jeffries. He still has time to capture two dips, which is better than most of us can do. Incidentally he is a pretty good backstop in the football line.
text: "Black" is "one of them things" in the art of Jim Jeffries. He still has time to capture two dips, which is better than most of us can do. Incidentally he is a pretty good backstop in the football line.

name: Owen, James Tuttle
description: This follower of the Medicos is never happier than when telling of his extensive travels to co-eds with a Mrs. before their names. The Medical Class hasn't a more popular boy than "Bloody," even though he is said to eat eleventeen biscuits every meal, when he can get them. Tulane catches him next year and then the mighty career is opened to him. He lost three eye teeth, and other things more substantial, when the Athletics beat the Giants.
text: This follower of the Medicos is never happier than when telling of his extensive travels to co-eds with a Mrs. before their names. The Medical Class hasn't a more popular boy than "Bloody," even though he is said to eat eleventeen biscuits every meal, when he can get them. Tulane catches him next year and then the mighty career is opened to him. He lost three eye teeth, and other things more substantial, when the Athletics beat the Giants.

name: Love, Harvey
description: Just turn back a page or two and read about "The Swamp Rabbit," add fifty pounds to that and you have this fellow. With such a name this Attala lad should win an Attala classic before the close of the year. One of the leaders not only of the Medical Class, but of the advance guard into the dining room, three times a day.
text: Just turn back a page or two and read about "The Swamp Rabbit," add fifty pounds to that and you have this fellow. With such a name this Attala lad should win an Attala classic before the close of the year. One of the leaders not only of the Medical Class, but of the advance guard into the dining room, three times a day.

name: Pool, Wm. Cam.
description: Pool is a good warm friend to his associates. He has always conducted himself in a dignified way, while at the University, and has won the respect and esteem of all those who know him. We hate to lose such as he.
text: Pool is a good warm friend to his associates. He has always conducted himself in a dignified way, while at the University, and has won the respect and esteem of all those who know him. We hate to lose such as he.

name: Ridgway, Ivison B.
major: B.S., Certificate in Medicine, Student Assistant in Anatomy, Millsaps College
description: He's a great big beautiful doll, a good doctor, and is never happier than when washing bottles as the Assistant in Anatomy. He blushed in sixteen original colors when a certain co-ed smiled at him. He is sure to make his mark as a surgeon even if it is on some helpless negro "stiff." Two hundred and forty pounds of good nature goes along with him. He is well liked by all his classmates.
text: He's a great big beautiful doll, a good doctor, and is never happier than when washing bottles as the Assistant in Anatomy. He blushed in sixteen original colors when a certain co-ed smiled at him. He is sure to make his mark as a surgeon even if it is on some helpless negro "stiff." Two hundred and forty pounds of good nature goes along with him. He is well liked by all his classmates.

name: Shipp, Cyrus M.
description: He is the biggest heart smasher in school, a railroad man—and tells jokes. We sometimes wonder if Cy thinks we believe those marvelous tales he tells, some of them are so fishy that they have gills. His weekly visit to Water Valley is getting serious. We don't know who she is, but the conductor has our best wishes. If success is assured to any of the young doctors, the good-natured, friendly, whole-souled son of sunshine is surely a member of that part of the class.
text: He is the biggest heart smasher in school, a railroad man—and tells jokes. We sometimes wonder if Cy thinks we believe those marvelous tales he tells, some of them are so fishy that they have gills. His weekly visit to Water Valley is getting serious. We don't know who she is, but the conductor has our best wishes. If success is assured to any of the young doctors, the good-natured, friendly, whole-souled son of sunshine is surely a member of that part of the class.

name: Tucker, William F.
description: Another member of that great club composed of citizens of Jones. He is better suited right now for athletics than for medicine, but he is studying hard to work the necessary change. Give him a baseball bat and a fulcrum and he can move the world while on the football field he has made the world move round for many opponents. J. M. A. sent him to us and we thank her.
text: Another member of that great club composed of citizens of Jones. He is better suited right now for athletics than for medicine, but he is studying hard to work the necessary change. Give him a baseball bat and a fulcrum and he can move the world while on the football field he has made the world move round for many opponents. J. M. A. sent him to us and we thank her.

name: Turkey, Hubert King
description: If "Doc" continues his hard work of the last two years, surely he will make a successful practicing physician. He spends from the early morn until late at night with his dissecting instruments and it is said that he enjoys the work. Although a very light weight, this lad because of his grit and assertiveness, won recognition on the gridiron where men of greater weight failed. He arrived from Castle Heights and though his stay with us numbers only two years, we like him and our best wishes follow him.
text: If "Doc" continues his hard work of the last two years, surely he will make a successful practicing physician. He spends from the early morn until late at night with his dissecting instruments and it is said that he enjoys the work. Although a very light weight, this lad because of his grit and assertiveness, won recognition on the gridiron where men of greater weight failed. He arrived from Castle Heights and though his stay with us numbers only two years, we like him and our best wishes follow him.

name: Rysdorpu, Vivian P.
description: "Monk" came to us from afar, but he is a Mississippian nevertheless. His ability and renown as an athlete has preceded this article and we shall refrain from singing his praises. As a practical joker he has no equal and we very much fear that he will "Prep" around the bedside of his patients, and if he does they will surely forsake their couches- the dead would flinch from such punishment. He may give up medicine for contracting and if he does, he will doubtless raise many sky-scrapers.

He may give up medicine for contracting and if he does, he will doubtless raise many sky-scrapers.

name: Anney, James Stuart
major: B.S., Ph.B., President Junior Ph.B. Class 1910-11, Phi Sigma
description: Two dips are too much for some men to carry away at once, but not for this cream of Pontotoc's products. Jim's ambition makes him live not for the present, but he keeps his eye on the future. This assures him success in his chosen profession. Having a talent for Chemistry and other by-products of pharmacy, success is doubly sure.
text: Two dips are too much for some men to carry away at once, but not for this cream of Pontotoc's products. Jim's ambition makes him live not for the present, but he keeps his eye on the future. This assures him success in his chosen profession. Having a talent for Chemistry and other by-products of pharmacy, success is doubly sure.

name: Carter, Claudius Aden
description: We confidently predict that the first vacancy in the State Pharmacy Board will be filled by the appointment of the illustrious man whose picture is herewith exposed to the public gaze. Even though he may try as hard as he can, success will surely overtake him. He is a quiet boy and unless you knew him you would never think that he was nearing the "goal."
text: We confidently predict that the first vacancy in the State Pharmacy Board will be filled by the appointment of the illustrious man whose picture is herewith exposed to the public gaze. Even though he may try as hard as he can, success will surely overtake him. He is a quiet boy and unless you knew him you would never think that he was nearing the "goal."

name: Chilton, Thomas Dudley
description: This lad has been with us a long time, before he went to California, and since he came back. The change did him good, but he still takes the Campus Course, and it requires just as much time for him to walk from the monument to James' corner as it did in days past. "Top" will be a great Pharmacist and some day he will have a drug store like our "Uncle" and we will pay for soda water for our children as our fathers have done.
text: This lad has been with us a long time, before he went to California, and since he came back. The change did him good, but he still takes the Campus Course, and it requires just as much time for him to walk from the monument to James' corner as it did in days past. "Top" will be a great Pharmacist and some day he will have a drug store like our "Uncle" and we will pay for soda water for our children as our fathers have done.

name: Fox, Newman Sherril
description: Some one has suggested that the gentleman is inclined to be lazy, but we will let you draw your own conclusion from the photo hereto attached. It is true that he is always uneasy, lest he break down his health; this is one of his original ideas. However, his ability is shown by the fact that he hasn't fallen by the wayside up to the present and that he will soon be turned out to mix physic for all who dare to "run the risk."

It is true that he is always uneasy, lest he break down his health; this is one of his original ideas. However, his ability is shown by the fact that he hasn't fallen by the wayside up to the present and that he will soon be turned out to mix physic for all who dare to "run the risk."

name: Goodman, Thomas E.
description: "Tommy" came to us from the strong county of Union, and at first we thought he was an "Innocent Abroad," but we soon realized our mistake, when his classmates began to report his doings over at the Science Hall—then when his grades came in, we knew that we had committed an unpardonable error. Some of his marks even went so near to the one-hundred mark as to almost catch fire—now we know him.
text: "Tommy" came to us from the strong county of Union, and at first we thought he was an "Innocent Abroad," but we soon realized our mistake, when his classmates began to report his doings over at the Science Hall—then when his grades came in, we knew that we had committed an unpardonable error. Some of his marks even went so near to the one-hundred mark as to almost catch fire—now we know him.

name: Hunt, Eustace J.
description: When the Pilgrims landed from the Mayflower, in 1620, it was confidently predicted that this young man would be a Pharmacist, by the Wise Ones, and they were correct. Dr. Faser has the reputation of sending out wonders, and Hunt is one of the most wondrous. 'Tis said by his classmates that he is one of the best students in the class.
text: When the Pilgrims landed from the Mayflower, in 1620, it was confidently predicted that this young man would be a Pharmacist, by the Wise Ones, and they were correct. Dr. Faser has the reputation of sending out wonders, and Hunt is one of the most wondrous. 'Tis said by his classmates that he is one of the best students in the class.

name: Jones, Jim
description: This innocent student invented "Casey Jones" and thereby won his fame as a poet and his class, recognizing merit, as they always do, elected him to write poetry for the "Pharmaseuts." His poetical nature has won him such fame that it is feared he will desert the "pill rolling job" and turn to the more lucrative one of writing poems, exclusively for the "Kossuth Kamera," the well known weekly journal of home life.
text: This innocent student invented "Casey Jones" and thereby won his fame as a poet and his class, recognizing merit, as they always do, elected him to write poetry for the "Pharmaseuts." His poetical nature has won him such fame that it is feared he will desert the "pill rolling job" and turn to the more lucrative one of writing poems, exclusively for the "Kossuth Kamera," the well known weekly journal of home life.

name: Mceachern, De Witt D.
description: Mc. is fortunate this year in having the honor to wag off from "Ole Miss" the degrees of U. M. A. A., Y. M. C. A. and Ph.G., all of which will be useful in his future profession. He is especially fond of Chemistry, says Dr. Faser, and this alone assures him of an abundant success in the future, when he undertakes to alleviate the illness of the good people of Carroll.
text: Mc. is fortunate this year in having the honor to wag off from "Ole Miss" the degrees of U. M. A. A., Y. M. C. A. and Ph.G., all of which will be useful in his future profession. He is especially fond of Chemistry, says Dr. Faser, and this alone assures him of an abundant success in the future, when he undertakes to alleviate the illness of the good people of Carroll.

name: Moore, Bertram Evans
description: His rosy cheeks and perfect complexion make one ask where the skirts and rats and puffs are, but with all of this he is a true disciple of Dr. Faser and makes some grades around the top. He will, no doubt, make an eminent pill-roller some day, after he has had sufficient experience to teach him the "fatality of overdoses."
text: His rosy cheeks and perfect complexion make one ask where the skirts and rats and puffs are, but with all of this he is a true disciple of Dr. Faser and makes some grades around the top. He will, no doubt, make an eminent pill-roller some day, after he has had sufficient experience to teach him the "fatality of overdoses."

name: Spann, Frank G.
description: Frankie's great knowledge of Chemistry has at last taught him the formula for water, but he wants to know to which the "2" belongs, the "H," or the "O," in "H2O." Frankie is really one of the best students in the Pharmacy Class, and his ability as a reserve baseball Highlandale southpaw will soon win him a place on the Reserve Giant team, if he does not get too interested in Pharmacy.
text: Frankie's great knowledge of Chemistry has at last taught him the formula for water, but he wants to know to which the "2" belongs, the "H," or the "O," in "H2O." Frankie is really one of the best students in the Pharmacy Class, and his ability as a reserve baseball Highlandale southpaw will soon win him a place on the Reserve Giant team, if he does not get too interested in Pharmacy.

name: Scarborough, Flora
description: Miss Flora tried every school in the state (nearly) before she came here, and even now sings I. I. & C. songs, and rahs for Mississippi College. She is not much given to studying, but knows how to bug profs and does not find studying essential. She has the distinction of being the first girl elected to class president, which honor is perhaps due to her campaign manager. Her life work will be among the heathen, that is if Master Cupid does not enter the stage.

name: Wood, Richard Tom
description: Everybody knows when Tom is near—his voice proclaims that fact in stentorian tones. If noise, and wit, and humor, and being won by widows, will make a successful pharmacist, Tom has his cinched. But he has more. Frequently he turns loose and leads his class. These make a rare combination for any boy. Everybody likes Tom.

If noise, and wit, and humor, and being won by widows, will make a successful pharmacist, Tom has his cinched. But he has more. Frequently he turns loose and leads his class. These make a rare combination for any boy. Everybody likes Tom.

name: Banks, Camille
description: She is nervous and worries a great deal over her work, but leaves this frame of mind behind when the time for play arrives. Her jolly nature, akin to the sunshine, spoken of by the poets, renders her very popular among co-eds, and with others.
text:
"Sorry" Hylsell— "I don't seem to remember."
